response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals with 15+ years of experience and solid architectural capability, they fall significantly short of critical minimum qualifications. Most critically, they have minimal C++ experience (approximately 1-2 years total) against the required 8 years, concentrated only at National Instruments (6 months) and bachelor thesis work. Additionally, their large-scale infrastructure experience is limited to mid-scale distributed systems (Dunnhumby's microservices), not production-scale cloud compute platforms. The recent move to a new role also provides limited visibility into sustained technical leadership in this domain.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship fundamentals with proven TDD, CI/CD, and quality practices across diverse technical domains
Staff-level experience demonstrating ability to own complex technical projects and drive them to completion
Demonstrated mentoring through workshops, code katas, and open-source community leadership
concerns: Critical gap: Only ~1-2 years of C++ experience versus required 8 years minimum (primarily 6 months at National Instruments)
Limited production-scale cloud architecture experience; background is primarily mid-scale distributed systems and various other technology stacks
No clear track record of 2+ years leading/mentoring cloud or DevOps-focused engineering teams in a technical leadership capacity
